CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-02-2023 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Entry Of Judgment Prove Up Hearing (Motions)
Off Calendar May-10-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar being set for motion for entry of judgment prove-up hearing.

Dept. 505; Judge Michelle Tong; Clerk: Shanika Monroe; Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 01:58:15 p.m. and concluding at 02:22:17 p.m.

Plaintiff Third 3101 LP, a California Limited Partnership represented by Stephanie Foster, Esq. via Zoom Video Conference.
Defendant Immersive Environments, LLC, a Delaware Limited Liability not appearing.

ZACK SPENCER is sworn to testify on behalf of Plaintiff.

Counsel for Plaintiff presents summary of case and an offer of proof.
Court examines Counsel for Plaintiff re: its request for attorney fees.

ZACK SPENCER is examined by the Court. Witness attests that comments made by Plaintiff's counsel are true and correct.

The following exhibits are MARKED/ADMITTED INTO EVIDENCE:

Plaintiff's Exhibit No. A - Redacted Complaint for Breach of Contract filed Jun-16-2022

The matter is submitted.

The Court determines the following:

1. Defendant was served personally on Jun-28-2022 and proof of service was filed on Jun-30-2022. Defendant was given the opportunity to be heard.
2. Default entered against Defendant Immersive Environments, LLC on Aug-03-2022 and request for entry of judgment was filed Aug-24-2022.
3. DOES 1-20 were DISMISSED on Feb-09-2023.

Based on the operative pleading filed Jun-16-2022, summary of the case, Declaration of Thomas Brown and testimony of Plaintiff's witness corroborating the Declaration of Alex Cartlidge filed Feb-09-2023, the Court finds plaintiff has proven essential elements in its complaint. The testimony of Zack Spencer is accepted into evidence. Court finds Defendant entered into a settlement agreement to pay rent and failed to do so. Defendant breached the terms of the settlement agreement.

Plaintiff withdraws it request for attorney fees.
Court shall award attorney fees for the time spent on preparing default package.
Court examines counsel for Plaintiff to calculate the sum of attorney fees.

Court grants pre-judgment interest for the period of Sep-15-2021 thru Nov-30-2022.

After consideration and good cause appearing, the Court RULES in favor of Plaintiff Third 3101 LP, a California Limited Partnership, and against Defendant Immersive Environments, LLC, a Delaware Limited Liability Company, as follows:

1. Damages: $36,149.27
2. Pre-Judgment Interest: $3,908.50
3. Costs: $1,427.54
4. Attorney Fees: $3,800.00

TOTAL JUDGMENT: $41,485.31
Proposed judgment to be submitted.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Seal Portions Of Complaint; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Feb-07-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF THIRD 3101 LP, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED PARTNERSHIP'S Motion To Seal Portions Of Complaint.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Matter is transferred to be heard in department 501 on the same date at 9:30 a.m. That department handles all landlord-tenant matters per SF Local Rule 8.10A1.

Judge: Richard Ulmer; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/RBU)

Plaintiff'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Seal Portions Of Complaint
Off Calendar Feb-07-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Plaintiff's Motion To Seal Portions Of Complaint

Plaintiff's Motion to Seal Portions of Complaint is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion is GRANTED as to paragraphs 11, 12, 13, 16, 17, and 19. Motion is DENIED as to paragraphs 14 and 20. Motion is also denied as to the request to seal the entirety of the Settlement Agreement without prejudice. This request is not narrowly tailored to protect parties' overriding interests.

Prevailing/Mooving party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Charles F. Haines, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (501/CFH)
